MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-20-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF PAUL W. GARRETT TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF GARRETT APPEARS TO HAVE ABANDONED HIS CLAIMS AND FAILED TO MAINTAIN CONTACT WITH HIS COUNSEL. GARRETT IS DIRECTED TO APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION WITHIN THIRTY DAYS OF THE ENTRY OF THE ORDER, ON NOT LESS THAN SEVEN DAYS' NOTICE, AND TO PAY DISCOVERY SANCTIONS OF $1,330 WITHIN 30 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER, UNLESS GARRETT'S CLAIMS ARE DISMISSED BEFORE THAT DATE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: ROGER MEAD, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Defendant 32-40 Sixth Street Llc, Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Paul W. Garrett To Appear For Deposition, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark D. Fenske, Esq. of VOGL MEREDITH BURKE LLP, Tel. 415.398.0200, appearing for the Defendants 32-40 Sixth Street, et al.
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem.

Ruling - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion to compel plaintiff Paul W. Garrett to appear for deposition is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff Garrett appears to have abandoned his claims and failed to maintain contact with his counsel. Garrett is directed to appear for his deposition within thirty days of the entry of the order, on not less than seven days' notice, and to pay discovery sanctions of $1,330 within 30 days of entry of the order, unless Garrett's claims are dismissed before that date. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT/HK)
